provider: API-Football (api-sports.io). Same API also resold on RapidAPI as "API-Football" by the api-sports provider.
authHeaderName: Direct api-sports.io: x-apisports-key: <YOUR_KEY>. Via RapidAPI: two headers instead — x-rapidapi-key: <YOUR_KEY> and x-rapidapi-host: api-football-v1.p.rapidapi.com
rateLimitNotes: Free tier: 100 requests per day and 10 requests per minute (per API-Football's rate-limit docs and FreeAPIHub summary). All endpoints are accessible on the free plan. Each response includes rate-limit headers: x-ratelimit-requests-limit (daily allocation), x-ratelimit-requests-remaining (remaining for the day), X-RateLimit-Limit (per-minute max), X-RateLimit-Remaining (per-minute remaining). Note: live/in-play data updates every ~15s, but at 100 req/day the free tier cannot poll live matches frequently — API-Football recommends ~1 call/min only while a fixture is in progress (which alone would exhaust the free daily quota in ~1.7 hours), otherwise 1 call/day. Exceeding the per-minute cap or generating abnormal traffic can get access throttled or blocked by their firewall without notice. For a real live companion app, a paid plan is effectively required; the free tier is suited to development/testing only. The exact free per-minute figure (10/min) is reported by secondary sources; confirm against your dashboard since plan terms can change.

endpoints.purpose: (a) Fixtures by league + season — full World Cup schedule (all 104 matches: date, kickoff time in UTC, venue, status, fixture_id, teams, score)
endpoints.exampleQuery: /fixtures?league=1&season=2026  (optionally add &round=Group%20A or &team={teamId} or &date=2026-06-25). Round names come from /fixtures/rounds?league=1&season=2026
endpoints.responseNotes: response[] array; each item has fixture{id,date,timestamp,venue{id,name,city},status{long,short,elapsed}}, league{id,season,round}, teams{home,away}, goals{home,away}, score{halftime,fulltime,extratime,penalty}. fixture.id is the master key used by all sub-endpoints below. response[].fixture.status.short = NS/1H/HT/2H/FT etc.

endpoints.exampleQuery: /fixtures?live=all  (all in-play matches) OR /fixtures?live=1 (in-play matches filtered to World Cup league id 1; multiple leagues dash-separated, e.g. live=1-2-3)
endpoints.responseNotes: Same fixture object shape as (a) but only currently in-play matches; status.elapsed gives current minute. Data refreshed roughly every 15 seconds upstream. Mind the free-tier quota when polling.

endpoints.purpose: (c) Fixture events — goals, cards, substitutions with the minute
endpoints.responseNotes: response[] array of events; each has time{elapsed,extra}, team{id,name}, player{id,name}, assist{id,name}, type (Goal/Card/subst/Var), detail (e.g. 'Normal Goal','Yellow Card','Red Card','Penalty'), comments. Use time.elapsed (+ time.extra for stoppage) for the minute.

endpoints.purpose: (d) Fixture statistics — possession, shots, etc. (per team)
endpoints.exampleQuery: /fixtures/statistics?fixture={fixtureId}  (optionally &team={teamId} for one side, &type=Ball%20Possession for a single stat)
endpoints.responseNotes: response[] is one entry per team; each has team{id,name} and statistics[] = array of {type,value} pairs. Types include 'Ball Possession' (e.g. '55%'), 'Shots on Goal','Total Shots','Shots off Goal','Blocked Shots','Fouls','Corner Kicks','Offsides','Yellow Cards','Red Cards','Passes %','expected_goals'. value may be string/number/null.

endpoints.purpose: (e) Standings — group tables (12 groups A–L for WC 2026 group stage)
endpoints.responseNotes: response[0].league.standings is an array of group tables (one inner array per group). Each row: rank, team{id,name,logo}, points, goalsDiff, group (e.g. 'Group A'), form, status, description (e.g. qualification note), all{played,win,draw,lose,goals{for,against}}, home{...}, away{...}, update timestamp.

endpoints.path: /players/squads  (quick full roster) — alternatively /players for paginated profiles+stats
endpoints.exampleQuery: /players/squads?team={teamId}  (current registered roster, names + player IDs; NO season param on this endpoint). For full profiles/stats with pagination: /players?team={teamId}&season=2026&page=1, or by competition /players?league=1&season=2026&page=1
endpoints.responseNotes: /players/squads: response[0].team{id,name} + players[] each {id,name,age,number,position,photo}. /players: paginated (response[].player{...bio...} + statistics[]); check the paging{current,total} object and loop pages — costs one request per page (watch the free quota).

endpoints.purpose: (g) Lineups by fixture — starting XI, formation, substitutes, coach
endpoints.responseNotes: response[] one entry per team; each has team{id,name,colors}, coach{id,name}, formation (e.g. '4-3-3'), startXI[] and substitutes[] where each is {player{id,name,number,pos,grid}}. grid gives pitch position (row:col). Available shortly before kickoff once lineups are published.
